{{nihongo|'''Taka Shrine'''|多珂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]].

database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県南相馬市原町区高城ノ内112', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Taka-jinja}} [[JA:多珂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11430895}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 多珂神社 |画像 = [[File:Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine in Shinto ritual season 1.JPG|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|福島県|Q71707}}{{jalink|南相馬市|Q642094}}{{jalink|原町区|Q24892679}}高城ノ内112 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|37|36|2.81|N|140|59|33.52|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=ti|name=多珂神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊邪那伎命}}（多珂荒御魂命） |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}）{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = 第12代{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}40年7月 |本殿 = 一間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 鷹大明神 |例祭 = {{jalink|4月18日|Q2521}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=多珂神社|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''多珂神社'''（たかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|福島県|Q71707}}{{jalink|南相馬市|Q642094}}{{jalink|原町区|Q24892679}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|県社}}。 === 祭神 === * '''{{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊邪那伎命}}''' *: 相馬藩内の郷土史資料である『奥相志』によれば、伊邪那伎命の{{jalink|荒魂・和魂|Q495568|荒魂}}である「多珂荒御魂命」を祀るとする。 神体は往古から拝観を拒まれているため不明。「鷹の像」ではないかという説があるが、この鷹の像は、社伝にある「白符の鷹」が神体であると誤って伝わったものだとされている。 === 歴史 === === 概史 === 多珂神社は、{{jalink|陸奥国|Q907389}}{{jalink|行方郡 (福島県)|Q11626139|行方郡}}に鎮座する{{jalink|式内社||延喜式内社}}八座（多珂神社、{{jalink|鹿島御子神社|Q11677124}}、{{jalink|御刀神社|Q11488802}}、{{jalink|益多嶺神社}}、{{jalink|日祭神社}}、{{jalink|高座神社|Q11669904}}、{{jalink|冠嶺神社|Q11395049}}、{{jalink|押雄神社}}）の首座にある。「多賀神社」の名を持つ神社の中で、全国で唯一の{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}の位が与えられている神社であり、東北地方の多賀神社は、当社を根源とするものだという。 由緒は不詳だが、創建は{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}40年に、東征のためにこの地を訪れた{{jalink|日本武尊}}により、戦勝祈願のために勧請されたと伝えられる。現在の鎮座地の北西にある古内地区には太田川が流れており、そこには「大明神河原」という場所があるという。往古の社地は大明神河原の南の丘陵地にあったと伝わる。{{jalink|仲哀天皇|Q179971}}7年2月、暴風雨により社殿が流されて大破し、近くの太田川の岸辺に漂着した。同年9月に芦野平（現在の鎮座地である城ノ内）に遷座した。 康暦2年({{jalink|1380年|Q6327}})には藤原吉守寄進の銘がある鰐口があり、本殿内陣に掛けられているという。{{jalink|承応|Q1145081}}四乙巳年({{jalink|1655年|Q6975}})4月から明暦元年(同年)には宮祠を修復し、{{jalink|相馬忠胤|Q8514579}}が木材を寄進した。{{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}年間には{{jalink|相馬昌胤|Q8514599}}が崇敬する{{jalink|吉田神道|Q1760351}}の神殿である「養真殿」を城内に建立、その際に故あって多珂神社へ「白符の鷹」の像を彫刻して奉納した。この「白符の鷹」の木像は厨子に納めて保管されていると伝わる。現在も「白符の鷹像」は社宝として本殿内に安置されており、社伝によれば「白符の鷹」という名称は、一見すると普通の木像だが、羽根の部分が光の当たり具合により白く見えるということから名付けられたという。 {{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}9年（{{jalink|1724年|Q6926}}）に、相馬昌胤が社田1石9斗余を寄進するなど、歴代相馬藩主からも篤い崇敬を受けた。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年({{jalink|1873年|Q7757}})に郷社に指定、{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}19年({{jalink|1944年|Q5268}})には県社へと定められた。 多珂神社は、古来より延命長寿・願望成就・家内安全・安産成就・海上安全・大漁満足などの御神徳があるとされるが、戦勝祈願のために勧請されたという由緒があるため、特に選挙での必勝祈願に訪れる崇敬者が多いという。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}15年（{{jalink|796年|Q33452}}）10月22日、従五位下 （『{{jalink|日本後紀|Q1077029}}』） - 表記は「多賀神」 === 境内 === === 社殿 === *本殿 - 一間社流造で、脇障子・勾欄付き。脇障子の部分には龍の彫刻がされている。 File:Honden of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|本殿 File:Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine in Shinto ritual season 2.JPG|拝殿 '''幣殿内部''' 幣殿の格子天井には動植物や人物などの彩色絵画が施されている。また、正月三が日の期間のみ幣殿の本殿御扉前に護摩壇が設置され、そこで護摩祈祷が執り行われる。護摩祈祷では、神札や授与品などを直接護摩の炎にかざしての祈願が行われる。 File:Heiden of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|幣殿内部 File:Pictures of Heiden of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|幣殿格子天井の絵画
写真奥が本殿御扉側。 File:Heiden of Taka-jinja shrine 2.JPG|幣殿格子天井の絵画
写真奥が幣殿入口。 File:Gomadan of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|多珂神社の護摩壇
正月期間のみ「ノデッポウ」と呼ばれる木を護摩木として使い護摩祈祷が行われる。 '''拝殿内部''' 拝殿内には、「伊弉諾尊と伊弉冉尊の絵馬」や「鷹」の絵画、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}38年に日露戦争での{{jalink|旅順攻囲戦|Q210800|旅順陥落}}を記念して奉納された「国家鎮護」の額などが納められている。 File:Inside of Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine 1.JPG|拝殿内部 File:Inside of Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine 2.JPG|拝殿内部・幣殿入口 File:Ema board of Izanagi and Izanami at Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|伊弉諾尊と伊弉冉尊の絵馬 File:Picture of Falcon inside of Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|鷹の絵画
別名「鷹大明神」と呼ばれるため鷹の絵が多く奉納されている。 File:Board of "spiritual protection of the state" at Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|「国家鎮護」祈願額 === その他建造物 === * 七福神の石像 * 本殿背後の神木のそばには、神仏習合時代にあった仏像が置かれている。 === 参道 === 参道には神池があり、中央の小島には子牛田山津見神社の小祠と金毘羅神社の石碑が鎮座している。第一鳥居は、{{jalink|磐城太田駅|Q6100384}}の南東、{{jalink|常磐線|Q1369263}}の線路沿いに立つ。 File:Kogota Yamatsumi-jinja shrine and Konpira-jinja shrine in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|子牛田山津見神社の小祠・金毘羅神社石碑 File:Main approach to Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|参道 File:Main approach to Taka-jinja shrine 2.JPG|多珂神社参道入口 File:Torii gate of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|第一鳥居 === 摂末社 === *八坂稲荷神社 *: 社殿は多珂神社の旧本殿を流用したもので、社殿の中には八坂神社と稲荷神社が納められている。八坂神社は以前は牛頭天王社と呼ばれており、現在の鎮座地である城ノ内地区に鎮座していた。『奥相志』の記録によれば、八坂神社の神体の長さは7寸である。稲荷神社は現在の鎮座地から南東の御稲荷地区にあったものを八坂神社へ合祀したという。 *: 社殿東側には、御神木の杉の木と雷神社、青麻大権現（青麻神社）石碑がある。 * 雷神社 *: 神体の長さは7寸と記録にある。 *青麻大権現（青麻神社）石碑 *: 祭神は天照大御神、月読神、天御中主神。宮城県仙台市宮城野区岩切の{{jalink|青麻神社|Q11662833}}から勧請されたものである。 File:Yasaka-Inari-jinja shrine in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|八坂稲荷神社（多珂神社旧本殿） File:Sanctified Japanese cedar tree and raijinja shrine in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|神木と雷神社 File:Raijinja shrine in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|雷神社 File:Aoso-jinja shrine in taka-jinja shrine.JPG|青麻大権現（青麻神社）の石碑 File:Statues of Shichifukujin in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|七福神の石像 File:Sanctified tree and Statue of Kannon in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|神木と仏像 === 貴船神社（多珂神社論社） === {{神社 |名称 = 貴船神社 |画像 = [[File:Haiden of Kifune-jinja shrine in Odaka ward,Minamisouma city. 01.JPG|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|福島県|Q71707}}{{jalink|南相馬市|Q642094}}{{jalink|小高区|Q24892654}}仲町2-50 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|37|33|59.4|N|140|59|23.5|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=貴船神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|淤加美神|Q644201|高龗神・闇龗神}}
{{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊弉諾尊}}（伝）
{{jalink|タカミムスビ||高皇産霊尊}}（伝）
蚕養明神（伝） |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}）{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}} |創建 = 不詳（{{jalink|元亨|Q509889}}3年（{{jalink|1323年|Q5771}}）以前） |本殿 = 一間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |例祭 = 1月第二日曜日 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=貴船神社|coordinates= {{coord2|37|33|59.4|N|140|59|23.5|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=290|coord={{coord|37|33|59.4|N|140|59|23.5|E}}}} }} 南相馬市小高区の相馬小高神社近くに鎮座する。祭神は高龗神・闇龗神である。 === 歴史 === 貴船神社の創建時期は不明だが、元亨3年（1323年）以前から信仰されていたとされ、『奥相志』によれば、神体を収めた箱には{{jalink|保延|Q1193929}}五己未年8月に造営されたという銘文があるという記述がある。なお、神体は「龍にまたがり白衣を着た像」であるとされる。{{jalink|文治|Q1194415}}5年に{{jalink|相馬師常|Q11582495}}が行方郡を領地とすると、貴船神社を郡内の総社とし、社殿の修飾を行うなど篤く信仰した。貴船神社は、主に祈雨・止雨、五穀豊穣、開運出世、武術の神として信仰されていた。 『奥相志』によれば、南小高邑の貴布禰（貴船）社内に多珂神社があったという。宮方二尺五寸の社殿であった。景行天皇の時代に勧請された神社で、祭神は伊弉諾尊と高皇産霊尊の二座、相殿神として蚕養明神を祀っていた。 文化12（1815年）乙亥年、相馬藩主である{{jalink|相馬益胤|Q8514605}}は、祠官社家と渡部美綱という者に行方郡内の式内社八座を調査し特定するよう命じた。渡辺美綱は、延喜式内名神大社とされる多珂神社は、高邑の光明寺境に鎮座する鷹大明神であると報告した。その報告に対し、小高邑の貴布禰社の祠官である高玉丹波という者が、多珂神社は小高邑に鎮座する小祠であると上稟した。 それによれば、多珂神社は三種の神器のうちの八尺瓊勾玉を地主神として勧請した神社であり、古より小高村の地主神であると報告した。祠官家の先祖は元々は「多珂玉」と名乗っていたが、多珂神社に憚って「高玉」と名乗るようになった。多賀信仰の総本社である滋賀県の{{jalink|多賀大社|Q402091}}は「お多賀さま」と呼ばれており、小高郷の名前は、鎮座する多珂神社を「御多珂」と称したことにちなむという。高玉氏は小高区の{{jalink|相馬小高神社}}近くに鎮座する貴船神社と多珂神社の祭祀を司っており、貴船神社から十間ほどの小高邑二本松という場所に多珂神社が鎮座していた。その後、元禄年間に起きた洪水で多珂神社は流されたが、農夫が水中から祠を拾い上げて貴船神社の境内に合祀された。 以上の小高邑の多珂神社に関する由緒を高玉丹波が書き記して藩へと報告された。しかし、『奥相志』の記者によれば、 *文化12年の藩による調査以前に行われた式内社比定調査では、小高邑の多珂神社は記録されていない *高邑の多珂神社の社家である島氏の記録は{{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}15年の社家名簿や正徳年間の寺社問好誌にはあるが、高玉氏の記録は{{jalink|寛保|Q1145798}}元年以降に出てくる など、高玉丹波の説は疑うべきところが多いとしている。 現在、貴船神社参道入口にある由緒書の石碑には、祭神は高龗神・闇龗神と記されており、多珂神社については同床同座で鎮座していると記述されている。 === 境内 === File:Precincts of Kifune-jinja,Minamisoma.JPG|境内 File:Haiden of Kifune-jinja after massive earthquake 11 March 2011.JPG|拝殿 File:Honden of Kifune-jinja after massive earthquake 11 March 2011.JPG|本殿
流造。 === 関連地 === 相馬地方には上記二社の多珂神社を含めて五社の多珂神社があるという。 File:Taka-jinja shrine at Suzumigaoka Hachiman-jinja shrine.JPG|相馬市・涼ヶ岡八幡神社の境内社「多珂神社」 === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * 多珂神社：{{jalink|福島県|Q71707}}{{jalink|南相馬市|Q642094}}{{jalink|原町区|Q24892679}}高城ノ内112| * 貴船神社：{{jalink|福島県|Q71707}}{{jalink|南相馬市|Q642094}}{{jalink|小高区|Q24892654}}仲町2-50 === 参考文献 === * 多珂神社参拝のしおり（多珂神社社務所） * 『奥相志（相馬市史4）』（相馬市、1969年） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/276003.html 多珂神社]（國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社|名たかしんしや]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]]

'''多珂神社'''（たかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|福島県|Q71707}}{{jalink|南相馬市|Q642094}}{{jalink|原町区|Q24892679}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|県社}}。 === 祭神 === * '''{{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊邪那伎命}}''' *: 相馬藩内の郷土史資料である『奥相志』によれば、伊邪那伎命の{{jalink|荒魂・和魂|Q495568|荒魂}}である「多珂荒御魂命」を祀るとする。 神体は往古から拝観を拒まれているため不明。「鷹の像」ではないかという説があるが、この鷹の像は、社伝にある「白符の鷹」が神体であると誤って伝わったものだとされている。 === 歴史 === === 概史 === 多珂神社は、{{jalink|陸奥国|Q907389}}{{jalink|行方郡 (福島県)|Q11626139|行方郡}}に鎮座する{{jalink|式内社||延喜式内社}}八座（多珂神社、{{jalink|鹿島御子神社|Q11677124}}、{{jalink|御刀神社|Q11488802}}、{{jalink|益多嶺神社}}、{{jalink|日祭神社}}、{{jalink|高座神社|Q11669904}}、{{jalink|冠嶺神社|Q11395049}}、{{jalink|押雄神社}}）の首座にある。「多賀神社」の名を持つ神社の中で、全国で唯一の{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}の位が与えられている神社であり、東北地方の多賀神社は、当社を根源とするものだという。 由緒は不詳だが、創建は{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}40年に、東征のためにこの地を訪れた{{jalink|日本武尊}}により、戦勝祈願のために勧請されたと伝えられる。現在の鎮座地の北西にある古内地区には太田川が流れており、そこには「大明神河原」という場所があるという。往古の社地は大明神河原の南の丘陵地にあったと伝わる。{{jalink|仲哀天皇|Q179971}}7年2月、暴風雨により社殿が流されて大破し、近くの太田川の岸辺に漂着した。同年9月に芦野平（現在の鎮座地である城ノ内）に遷座した。 康暦2年({{jalink|1380年|Q6327}})には藤原吉守寄進の銘がある鰐口があり、本殿内陣に掛けられているという。{{jalink|承応|Q1145081}}四乙巳年({{jalink|1655年|Q6975}})4月から明暦元年(同年)には宮祠を修復し、{{jalink|相馬忠胤|Q8514579}}が木材を寄進した。{{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}年間には{{jalink|相馬昌胤|Q8514599}}が崇敬する{{jalink|吉田神道|Q1760351}}の神殿である「養真殿」を城内に建立、その際に故あって多珂神社へ「白符の鷹」の像を彫刻して奉納した。この「白符の鷹」の木像は厨子に納めて保管されていると伝わる。現在も「白符の鷹像」は社宝として本殿内に安置されており、社伝によれば「白符の鷹」という名称は、一見すると普通の木像だが、羽根の部分が光の当たり具合により白く見えるということから名付けられたという。 {{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}9年（{{jalink|1724年|Q6926}}）に、相馬昌胤が社田1石9斗余を寄進するなど、歴代相馬藩主からも篤い崇敬を受けた。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年({{jalink|1873年|Q7757}})に郷社に指定、{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}19年({{jalink|1944年|Q5268}})には県社へと定められた。 多珂神社は、古来より延命長寿・願望成就・家内安全・安産成就・海上安全・大漁満足などの御神徳があるとされるが、戦勝祈願のために勧請されたという由緒があるため、特に選挙での必勝祈願に訪れる崇敬者が多いという。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}15年（{{jalink|796年|Q33452}}）10月22日、従五位下 （『{{jalink|日本後紀|Q1077029}}』） - 表記は「多賀神」 === 境内 === === 社殿 === *本殿 - 一間社流造で、脇障子・勾欄付き。脇障子の部分には龍の彫刻がされている。 File:Honden of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|本殿 File:Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine in Shinto ritual season 2.JPG|拝殿 '''幣殿内部''' 幣殿の格子天井には動植物や人物などの彩色絵画が施されている。また、正月三が日の期間のみ幣殿の本殿御扉前に護摩壇が設置され、そこで護摩祈祷が執り行われる。護摩祈祷では、神札や授与品などを直接護摩の炎にかざしての祈願が行われる。 File:Heiden of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|幣殿内部 File:Pictures of Heiden of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|幣殿格子天井の絵画
写真奥が本殿御扉側。 File:Heiden of Taka-jinja shrine 2.JPG|幣殿格子天井の絵画
写真奥が幣殿入口。 File:Gomadan of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|多珂神社の護摩壇
正月期間のみ「ノデッポウ」と呼ばれる木を護摩木として使い護摩祈祷が行われる。 '''拝殿内部''' 拝殿内には、「伊弉諾尊と伊弉冉尊の絵馬」や「鷹」の絵画、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}38年に日露戦争での{{jalink|旅順攻囲戦|Q210800|旅順陥落}}を記念して奉納された「国家鎮護」の額などが納められている。 File:Inside of Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine 1.JPG|拝殿内部 File:Inside of Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine 2.JPG|拝殿内部・幣殿入口 File:Ema board of Izanagi and Izanami at Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|伊弉諾尊と伊弉冉尊の絵馬 File:Picture of Falcon inside of Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|鷹の絵画
別名「鷹大明神」と呼ばれるため鷹の絵が多く奉納されている。 File:Board of "spiritual protection of the state" at Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|「国家鎮護」祈願額 === その他建造物 === * 七福神の石像 * 本殿背後の神木のそばには、神仏習合時代にあった仏像が置かれている。 === 参道 === 参道には神池があり、中央の小島には子牛田山津見神社の小祠と金毘羅神社の石碑が鎮座している。第一鳥居は、{{jalink|磐城太田駅|Q6100384}}の南東、{{jalink|常磐線|Q1369263}}の線路沿いに立つ。 File:Kogota Yamatsumi-jinja shrine and Konpira-jinja shrine in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|子牛田山津見神社の小祠・金毘羅神社石碑 File:Main approach to Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|参道 File:Main approach to Taka-jinja shrine 2.JPG|多珂神社参道入口 File:Torii gate of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|第一鳥居 === 摂末社 === *八坂稲荷神社 *: 社殿は多珂神社の旧本殿を流用したもので、社殿の中には八坂神社と稲荷神社が納められている。八坂神社は以前は牛頭天王社と呼ばれており、現在の鎮座地である城ノ内地区に鎮座していた。『奥相志』の記録によれば、八坂神社の神体の長さは7寸である。稲荷神社は現在の鎮座地から南東の御稲荷地区にあったものを八坂神社へ合祀したという。 *: 社殿東側には、御神木の杉の木と雷神社、青麻大権現（青麻神社）石碑がある。 * 雷神社 *: 神体の長さは7寸と記録にある。 *青麻大権現（青麻神社）石碑 *: 祭神は天照大御神、月読神、天御中主神。宮城県仙台市宮城野区岩切の{{jalink|青麻神社|Q11662833}}から勧請されたものである。 File:Yasaka-Inari-jinja shrine in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|八坂稲荷神社（多珂神社旧本殿） File:Sanctified Japanese cedar tree and raijinja shrine in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|神木と雷神社 File:Raijinja shrine in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|雷神社 File:Aoso-jinja shrine in taka-jinja shrine.JPG|青麻大権現（青麻神社）の石碑 File:Statues of Shichifukujin in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|七福神の石像 File:Sanctified tree and Statue of Kannon in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|神木と仏像 === 貴船神社（多珂神社論社） === {{神社 |名称 = 貴船神社 |画像 = [[File:Haiden of Kifune-jinja shrine in Odaka ward,Minamisouma city. 01.JPG|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|福島県|Q71707}}{{jalink|南相馬市|Q642094}}{{jalink|小高区|Q24892654}}仲町2-50 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|37|33|59.4|N|140|59|23.5|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=貴船神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|淤加美神|Q644201|高龗神・闇龗神}}
{{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊弉諾尊}}（伝）
{{jalink|タカミムスビ||高皇産霊尊}}（伝）
蚕養明神（伝） |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}）{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}} |創建 = 不詳（{{jalink|元亨|Q509889}}3年（{{jalink|1323年|Q5771}}）以前） |本殿 = 一間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |例祭 = 1月第二日曜日 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=貴船神社|coordinates= {{coord2|37|33|59.4|N|140|59|23.5|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=290|coord={{coord|37|33|59.4|N|140|59|23.5|E}}}} }} 南相馬市小高区の相馬小高神社近くに鎮座する。祭神は高龗神・闇龗神である。 === 歴史 === 貴船神社の創建時期は不明だが、元亨3年（1323年）以前から信仰されていたとされ、『奥相志』によれば、神体を収めた箱には{{jalink|保延|Q1193929}}五己未年8月に造営されたという銘文があるという記述がある。なお、神体は「龍にまたがり白衣を着た像」であるとされる。{{jalink|文治|Q1194415}}5年に{{jalink|相馬師常|Q11582495}}が行方郡を領地とすると、貴船神社を郡内の総社とし、社殿の修飾を行うなど篤く信仰した。貴船神社は、主に祈雨・止雨、五穀豊穣、開運出世、武術の神として信仰されていた。 『奥相志』によれば、南小高邑の貴布禰（貴船）社内に多珂神社があったという。宮方二尺五寸の社殿であった。景行天皇の時代に勧請された神社で、祭神は伊弉諾尊と高皇産霊尊の二座、相殿神として蚕養明神を祀っていた。 文化12（1815年）乙亥年、相馬藩主である{{jalink|相馬益胤|Q8514605}}は、祠官社家と渡部美綱という者に行方郡内の式内社八座を調査し特定するよう命じた。渡辺美綱は、延喜式内名神大社とされる多珂神社は、高邑の光明寺境に鎮座する鷹大明神であると報告した。その報告に対し、小高邑の貴布禰社の祠官である高玉丹波という者が、多珂神社は小高邑に鎮座する小祠であると上稟した。 それによれば、多珂神社は三種の神器のうちの八尺瓊勾玉を地主神として勧請した神社であり、古より小高村の地主神であると報告した。祠官家の先祖は元々は「多珂玉」と名乗っていたが、多珂神社に憚って「高玉」と名乗るようになった。多賀信仰の総本社である滋賀県の{{jalink|多賀大社|Q402091}}は「お多賀さま」と呼ばれており、小高郷の名前は、鎮座する多珂神社を「御多珂」と称したことにちなむという。高玉氏は小高区の{{jalink|相馬小高神社}}近くに鎮座する貴船神社と多珂神社の祭祀を司っており、貴船神社から十間ほどの小高邑二本松という場所に多珂神社が鎮座していた。その後、元禄年間に起きた洪水で多珂神社は流されたが、農夫が水中から祠を拾い上げて貴船神社の境内に合祀された。 以上の小高邑の多珂神社に関する由緒を高玉丹波が書き記して藩へと報告された。しかし、『奥相志』の記者によれば、 *文化12年の藩による調査以前に行われた式内社比定調査では、小高邑の多珂神社は記録されていない *高邑の多珂神社の社家である島氏の記録は{{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}15年の社家名簿や正徳年間の寺社問好誌にはあるが、高玉氏の記録は{{jalink|寛保|Q1145798}}元年以降に出てくる など、高玉丹波の説は疑うべきところが多いとしている。 現在、貴船神社参道入口にある由緒書の石碑には、祭神は高龗神・闇龗神と記されており、多珂神社については同床同座で鎮座していると記述されている。 === 境内 === File:Precincts of Kifune-jinja,Minamisoma.JPG|境内 File:Haiden of Kifune-jinja after massive earthquake 11 March 2011.JPG|拝殿 File:Honden of Kifune-jinja after massive earthquake 11 March 2011.JPG|本殿
流造。 === 関連地 === 相馬地方には上記二社の多珂神社を含めて五社の多珂神社があるという。 File:Taka-jinja shrine at Suzumigaoka Hachiman-jinja shrine.JPG|相馬市・涼ヶ岡八幡神社の境内社「多珂神社」 === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * 多珂神社：{{jalink|福島県|Q71707}}{{jalink|南相馬市|Q642094}}{{jalink|原町区|Q24892679}}高城ノ内112| * 貴船神社：{{jalink|福島県|Q71707}}{{jalink|南相馬市|Q642094}}{{jalink|小高区|Q24892654}}仲町2-50 === 参考文献 === * 多珂神社参拝のしおり（多珂神社社務所） * 『奥相志（相馬市史4）』（相馬市、1969年） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/276003.html 多珂神社]（國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社|名たかしんしや]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県南相馬市原町区高城ノ内112', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Taka-jinja}} [[JA:多珂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11430895}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Taka Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine in Shinto ritual season 1.JPG|270px]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisōma City|Q642094}}{{jalink|Haramachi Ward|Q24892679}}112 Takajō-no-uchi |Coordinates = {{Wikicoord2 DMS|37|36|2.81|N|140|59|33.52|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=ti|name=Taka Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} (Taka Ara-Mitama-no-Mikoto) |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|Myōjin Taisha}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}
Former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |Founded = 40th year of Emperor Keikō (12th Emperor), 7th month |Main hall = One-bay {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Alternate name = Taka Daimyōjin |Annual festival = {{jalink|April 18|Q2521}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Taka Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Taka Shrine''' (たかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Haramachi Ward|Q24892679}}, {{jalink|Minamisōma City|Q642094}}, {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}, and under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|former shrine rank}} it was designated as a {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. === Enshrined Deity === * '''{{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}''' *: According to *Ōsōshi*, a local historical record of the Sōma Domain, the shrine enshrines “Taka Ara-Mitama-no-Mikoto,” the {{jalink|Ara-mitama and Nigi-mitama|Q495568|Ara-mitama}} (rough spirit) of Izanagi-no-Mikoto. The shintai (sacred object) has been prohibited from being viewed since ancient times and is therefore unknown. There is a theory that it may be a “hawk statue,” but this hawk statue is said to be the result of a mistaken transmission identifying the “White-Token Hawk” mentioned in shrine tradition as the shintai. === History === === Overview === Taka Shrine is the foremost of the eight {{jalink|Shikinaisha||Engishiki-listed shrines}} in {{jalink|Namekata District, Fukushima|Q11626139}}, {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}} (Taka Shrine, {{jalink|Kashima Miko Shrine|Q11677124}}, {{jalink|Mitachi Shrine|Q11488802}}, {{jalink|Masutamine Shrine}}, {{jalink|Himatsuri Shrine}}, {{jalink|Takakura Shrine|Q11669904}}, {{jalink|Kanmuri Mine Shrine|Q11395049}}, {{jalink|Oshio Shrine}}). Among shrines bearing the name “Taga Shrine,” it is the only one nationwide to have been granted the rank of {{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964}}, and it is said that Taga Shrines throughout the Tōhoku region originate from this shrine. Although its exact origins are unknown, tradition holds that the shrine was founded in the 40th year of {{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}} by {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}}, who visited this area during his eastern campaign and enshrined the deity to pray for victory in battle. To the northwest of the current location lies the Furuuchi area, through which the Ōta River flows, and there is said to be a place called “Daimyōjin Kawara.” The ancient shrine site is said to have been on the southern hills of Daimyōjin Kawara. In the 7th year, 2nd month of {{jalink|Emperor Chūai|Q179971}}, a violent storm washed away and severely damaged the shrine buildings, which drifted to the banks of the nearby Ōta River. In the 9th month of the same year, the shrine was relocated to Ashinodaira (present-day Jō-no-uchi, the current site). In Kōryaku 2 ({{jalink|1380|Q6327}}), there existed a bronze bell (waniguchi) bearing an inscription of donation by Fujiwara Yoshimori, which is said to hang in the inner sanctuary of the main hall. From April of Jōō 4, Kinoe-mi year ({{jalink|1655|Q6975}}) to Meireki 1 (the same year), the shrine sanctuary was repaired, with timber donated by {{jalink|Sōma Tadatane|Q8514579}}. During the {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} era, {{jalink|Sōma Masatane|Q8514599}} built a sanctuary called “Yōshinden,” associated with {{jalink|Yoshida Shintō|Q1760351}}, within the castle grounds, and at that time, for certain reasons, had a statue of the “White-Token Hawk” carved and dedicated to Taka Shrine. This wooden statue of the “White-Token Hawk” is said to have been stored in a miniature shrine. Even today, the “White-Token Hawk Statue” is enshrined in the main hall as a sacred treasure. According to shrine tradition, although it appears to be an ordinary wooden statue, it was named “White-Token Hawk” because the feathers appear white depending on how the light strikes them. In Kyōhō 9 ({{jalink|1724|Q6926}}), Sōma Masatane donated shrine lands amounting to 1 koku and 9 to, among other offerings, and the shrine received deep reverence from successive lords of the Sōma Domain. In Meiji 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was designated a village shrine, and in Shōwa 19 ({{jalink|1944|Q5268}}), it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. Taka Shrine has long been believed to bestow divine benefits such as longevity, fulfillment of wishes, household safety, safe childbirth, maritime safety, and abundant catches. Because it was originally enshrined to pray for victory in battle, many worshippers in particular visit to pray for certain victory in elections. === Court Rank === * Enryaku 15 ({{jalink|796|Q33452}}) 10th month, 22nd day: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (*{{jalink|Nihon Kōki|Q1077029}}*) – recorded as “Taga Shrine.” === Shrine Grounds === === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Built in one-bay nagare-zukuri style, with side screens and balustrades. Dragon carvings are applied to the side screens. File:Honden of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Main Hall File:Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine in Shinto ritual season 2.JPG|Worship Hall '''Interior of the Heiden''' The coffered lattice ceiling of the heiden is decorated with polychrome paintings of animals, plants, and human figures. Only during the first three days of the New Year, a goma altar is set up in front of the main hall doors within the heiden, where goma fire rituals are performed. During these rituals, prayers are offered by holding amulets and votive items directly over the sacred flames. File:Heiden of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Interior of the Heiden File:Pictures of Heiden of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Paintings on the lattice ceiling of the Heiden
The rear of the photo shows the side of the main hall doors. File:Heiden of Taka-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Paintings on the lattice ceiling of the Heiden
The rear of the photo shows the Heiden entrance. File:Gomadan of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Goma altar at Taka Shrine
Only during the New Year period, a tree called “Nodeppō” is used as goma sticks for the ritual. '''Interior of the Worship Hall''' Inside the worship hall are housed items such as an ema depicting “Izanagi-no-Mikoto and Izanami-no-Mikoto,” paintings of hawks, and a plaque reading “Protection of the State,” dedicated in Meiji 38 to commemorate the {{jalink|Siege of Port Arthur|Q210800|Fall of Port Arthur}} during the Russo-Japanese War. File:Inside of Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine 1.JPG|Interior of the Worship Hall File:Inside of Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Interior of the Worship Hall / Entrance to the Heiden File:Ema board of Izanagi and Izanami at Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Ema of Izanagi-no-Mikoto and Izanami-no-Mikoto File:Picture of Falcon inside of Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Hawk painting
Many hawk images are dedicated because the shrine is also called “Taka Daimyōjin.” File:Board of "spiritual protection of the state" at Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|“Protection of the State” votive plaque === Other Structures === * Stone statues of the Seven Lucky Gods * Near the sacred tree behind the main hall, Buddhist statues from the era of syncretism between Shinto and Buddhism are placed. === Approach === Along the approach is a sacred pond, and on the small central island stand a small shrine of Kogota Yamatsumi Shrine and a stone monument of Konpira Shrine. The first torii gate stands southeast of {{jalink|Iwaki-Ōta Station|Q6100384}}, along the tracks of the {{jalink|Jōban Line|Q1369263}}. File:Kogota Yamatsumi-jinja shrine and Konpira-jinja shrine in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Small shrine of Kogota Yamatsumi Shrine and stone monument of Konpira Shrine File:Main approach to Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Approach File:Main approach to Taka-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Entrance to the approach of Taka Shrine File:Torii gate of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|First torii gate === Subsidiary and Branch Shrines === * Yasaka-Inari Shrine *: The shrine building reuses the former main hall of Taka Shrine, and within it are enshrined Yasaka Shrine and Inari Shrine. Yasaka Shrine was formerly called Gozu Tennō Shrine and had been located in the Jō-no-uchi area, the present location. According to *Ōsōshi*, the sacred object of Yasaka Shrine measures 7 sun in length. Inari Shrine was formerly located in the Oinari area southeast of the current site and was later enshrined together with Yasaka Shrine. *: On the east side of the shrine building are a sacred cedar tree, Raijin Shrine, and a stone monument of Aomasa Daigongen (Aomasa Shrine). * Raijin Shrine *: The sacred object is recorded as measuring 7 sun in length. * Aomasa Daigongen (Aomasa Shrine) Stone Monument *: The enshrined deities are Amaterasu Ōmikami, Tsukuyomi-no-Kami, and Ame-no-Minakanushi-no-Kami. It was transferred from {{jalink|Aomasa Shrine|Q11662833}} in Iwakiri, Miyagino Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture. File:Yasaka-Inari-jinja shrine in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Yasaka-Inari Shrine (former main hall of Taka Shrine) File:Sanctified Japanese cedar tree and raijinja shrine in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Sacred tree and Raijin Shrine File:Raijinja shrine in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Raijin Shrine File:Aoso-jinja shrine in taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone monument of Aomasa Daigongen (Aomasa Shrine) File:Statues of Shichifukujin in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone statues of the Seven Lucky Gods File:Sanctified tree and Statue of Kannon in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Sacred tree and Buddhist statue === Kifune Shrine (Ronsha of Taka Shrine) === {{Shrine |Name = Kifune Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Kifune-jinja shrine in Odaka ward,Minamisouma city. 01.JPG|270px]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisōma City|Q642094}}{{jalink|Odaka Ward|Q24892654}}2-50 Nakamachi |Coordinates = {{Wikicoord2 DMS|37|33|59.4|N|140|59|23.5|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=Kifune Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|Takaokami-no-Kami / Yamiokami-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} (trad.)
{{jalink|Takami-Musubi||Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}} (trad.)
Koyō Myōjin (trad.) |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|Myōjin Taisha}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}} |Founded = Unknown (before Genkō 3 ({{jalink|1323|Q5771}})) |Main hall = One-bay {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Annual festival = Second Sunday of January |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Kifune Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|37|33|59.4|N|140|59|23.5|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=290|coord={{coord|37|33|59.4|N|140|59|23.5|E}}}} }} It is enshrined near Sōma Odaka Shrine in Odaka Ward, Minamisōma City. The enshrined deities are Takaokami-no-Kami and Yamiokami-no-Kami. === History === The founding date of Kifune Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been worshipped since before Genkō 3 (1323). According to *Ōsōshi*, there is a record stating that the box housing the sacred object bears an inscription indicating it was made in the 5th year of Hōen, kinoe-mi year, 8th month. The sacred object is said to be a statue “riding a dragon and wearing white robes.” In Bunji 5, when {{jalink|Sōma Moritsune|Q11582495}} took control of Namekata District, he designated Kifune Shrine as the district’s chief shrine and deeply revered it, carrying out embellishments of the shrine buildings. Kifune Shrine was mainly worshipped as a deity of rainmaking and stopping rain, bountiful harvests, success and advancement in life, and martial arts. According to *Ōsōshi*, Taka Shrine once existed within the precincts of Kibune (Kifune) Shrine in Minami-Odaka Village. It was a shrine building measuring 2 shaku 5 sun in depth. It was a shrine enshrined during the era of Emperor Keikō, with Izanagi-no-Mikoto and Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto as the two main deities, and Koyō Myōjin as an accompanying deity. In Bunka 12 (1815), the lord of the Sōma Domain, {{jalink|Sōma Masutane|Q8514605}}, ordered shrine priests and a man named Watanabe Yoshitsuna to investigate and identify the eight Shikinaisha in Namekata District. Watanabe Yoshitsuna reported that the Engishiki-listed Myōjin Taisha known as Taka Shrine was the Taka Daimyōjin enshrined within the grounds of Kōmyō-ji Temple in Taka Village. In response, Takadama Tanba, a shrine priest of the Kibune Shrine in Odaka Village, submitted a counter-report claiming that Taka Shrine was a small shrine enshrined in Odaka Village. According to that report, Taka Shrine was a shrine where the Yasakani no Magatama, one of the Three Sacred Treasures, was enshrined as the local tutelary deity, and it had long been the guardian deity of Odaka Village. The ancestors of the shrine priest family originally bore the name “Takadama,” but out of deference to Taka Shrine they changed it to “Takadama.” The Taga faith’s head shrine, {{jalink|Taga Taisha|Q402091}} in Shiga Prefecture, is called “O-Taga-sama,” and the name Odaka is said to derive from calling the enshrined Taka Shrine “Mi-Taka.” The Takadama family administered the rites of both Kifune Shrine and Taka Shrine, which were enshrined near {{jalink|Sōma Odaka Shrine}} in Odaka Ward. Taka Shrine was located at a place called Nihonmatsu in Odaka Village, about ten ken from Kifune Shrine. Later, Taka Shrine was washed away by a flood during the Genroku era, but a farmer recovered the small shrine from the water and it was enshrined together within the precincts of Kifune Shrine. Takadama Tanba wrote down the above origins of Taka Shrine in Odaka Village and reported them to the domain. However, according to the compiler of *Ōsōshi*: * In shrine identification surveys conducted before the domain’s Bunka 12 investigation, Taka Shrine of Odaka Village is not recorded. * Records of the Shima family, shrine priests of Taka Shrine in Taka Village, appear in shrine priest registries from Genroku 15 and in temple and shrine surveys from the Shōtoku era, whereas records of the Takadama family only appear from Kanpō 1 onward. For these reasons, the theory of Takadama Tanba is said to contain many doubtful points. At present, the inscription on the stone monument describing the shrine’s origins at the entrance of Kifune Shrine’s approach states that the enshrined deities are Takaokami-no-Kami and Yamiokami-no-Kami, and that Taka Shrine is enshrined together in the same sanctuary. === Shrine Grounds === File:Precincts of Kifune-jinja,Minamisoma.JPG|Shrine grounds File:Haiden of Kifune-jinja after massive earthquake 11 March 2011.JPG|Worship Hall File:Honden of Kifune-jinja after massive earthquake 11 March 2011.JPG|Main Hall
Nagare-zukuri style. === Related Sites === In the Sōma region, there are said to be five Taka Shrines in total, including the two shrines described above. File:Taka-jinja shrine at Suzumigaoka Hachiman-jinja shrine.JPG|Taka Shrine, a precinct shrine within Suzumigaoka Hachiman Shrine in Sōma City === On-site Information === '''Locations''' * Taka Shrine: {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisōma City|Q642094}}{{jalink|Haramachi Ward|Q24892679}}112 Takajō-no-uchi * Kifune Shrine: {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisōma City|Q642094}}{{jalink|Odaka Ward|Q24892654}}2-50 Nakamachi === References === * Guide booklet for visiting Taka Shrine (Taka Shrine Office) * *Ōsōshi (History of Sōma City, Vol. 4)* (Sōma City, 1969) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/276003.html Taka Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation”) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Taka Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社|名たかしんしや]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{wikidata link|Q11430895}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Taka Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine in Shinto ritual season 1.JPG|270px]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisōma City|Q642094}}{{jalink|Haramachi Ward|Q24892679}}112 Takajō-no-uchi |Coordinates = {{Wikicoord2 DMS|37|36|2.81|N|140|59|33.52|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=ti|name=Taka Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} (Taka Ara-Mitama-no-Mikoto) |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|Myōjin Taisha}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}
Former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |Founded = 40th year of Emperor Keikō (12th Emperor), 7th month |Main hall = One-bay {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Alternate name = Taka Daimyōjin |Annual festival = {{jalink|April 18|Q2521}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Taka Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Taka Shrine''' (たかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Haramachi Ward|Q24892679}}, {{jalink|Minamisōma City|Q642094}}, {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}, and under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|former shrine rank}} it was designated as a {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. === Enshrined Deity === * '''{{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}''' *: According to *Ōsōshi*, a local historical record of the Sōma Domain, the shrine enshrines “Taka Ara-Mitama-no-Mikoto,” the {{jalink|Ara-mitama and Nigi-mitama|Q495568|Ara-mitama}} (rough spirit) of Izanagi-no-Mikoto. The shintai (sacred object) has been prohibited from being viewed since ancient times and is therefore unknown. There is a theory that it may be a “hawk statue,” but this hawk statue is said to be the result of a mistaken transmission identifying the “White-Token Hawk” mentioned in shrine tradition as the shintai. === History === === Overview === Taka Shrine is the foremost of the eight {{jalink|Shikinaisha||Engishiki-listed shrines}} in {{jalink|Namekata District, Fukushima|Q11626139}}, {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}} (Taka Shrine, {{jalink|Kashima Miko Shrine|Q11677124}}, {{jalink|Mitachi Shrine|Q11488802}}, {{jalink|Masutamine Shrine}}, {{jalink|Himatsuri Shrine}}, {{jalink|Takakura Shrine|Q11669904}}, {{jalink|Kanmuri Mine Shrine|Q11395049}}, {{jalink|Oshio Shrine}}). Among shrines bearing the name “Taga Shrine,” it is the only one nationwide to have been granted the rank of {{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964}}, and it is said that Taga Shrines throughout the Tōhoku region originate from this shrine. Although its exact origins are unknown, tradition holds that the shrine was founded in the 40th year of {{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}} by {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}}, who visited this area during his eastern campaign and enshrined the deity to pray for victory in battle. To the northwest of the current location lies the Furuuchi area, through which the Ōta River flows, and there is said to be a place called “Daimyōjin Kawara.” The ancient shrine site is said to have been on the southern hills of Daimyōjin Kawara. In the 7th year, 2nd month of {{jalink|Emperor Chūai|Q179971}}, a violent storm washed away and severely damaged the shrine buildings, which drifted to the banks of the nearby Ōta River. In the 9th month of the same year, the shrine was relocated to Ashinodaira (present-day Jō-no-uchi, the current site). In Kōryaku 2 ({{jalink|1380|Q6327}}), there existed a bronze bell (waniguchi) bearing an inscription of donation by Fujiwara Yoshimori, which is said to hang in the inner sanctuary of the main hall. From April of Jōō 4, Kinoe-mi year ({{jalink|1655|Q6975}}) to Meireki 1 (the same year), the shrine sanctuary was repaired, with timber donated by {{jalink|Sōma Tadatane|Q8514579}}. During the {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} era, {{jalink|Sōma Masatane|Q8514599}} built a sanctuary called “Yōshinden,” associated with {{jalink|Yoshida Shintō|Q1760351}}, within the castle grounds, and at that time, for certain reasons, had a statue of the “White-Token Hawk” carved and dedicated to Taka Shrine. This wooden statue of the “White-Token Hawk” is said to have been stored in a miniature shrine. Even today, the “White-Token Hawk Statue” is enshrined in the main hall as a sacred treasure. According to shrine tradition, although it appears to be an ordinary wooden statue, it was named “White-Token Hawk” because the feathers appear white depending on how the light strikes them. In Kyōhō 9 ({{jalink|1724|Q6926}}), Sōma Masatane donated shrine lands amounting to 1 koku and 9 to, among other offerings, and the shrine received deep reverence from successive lords of the Sōma Domain. In Meiji 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was designated a village shrine, and in Shōwa 19 ({{jalink|1944|Q5268}}), it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. Taka Shrine has long been believed to bestow divine benefits such as longevity, fulfillment of wishes, household safety, safe childbirth, maritime safety, and abundant catches. Because it was originally enshrined to pray for victory in battle, many worshippers in particular visit to pray for certain victory in elections. === Court Rank === * Enryaku 15 ({{jalink|796|Q33452}}) 10th month, 22nd day: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (*{{jalink|Nihon Kōki|Q1077029}}*) – recorded as “Taga Shrine.” === Shrine Grounds === === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Built in one-bay nagare-zukuri style, with side screens and balustrades. Dragon carvings are applied to the side screens. File:Honden of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Main Hall File:Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine in Shinto ritual season 2.JPG|Worship Hall '''Interior of the Heiden''' The coffered lattice ceiling of the heiden is decorated with polychrome paintings of animals, plants, and human figures. Only during the first three days of the New Year, a goma altar is set up in front of the main hall doors within the heiden, where goma fire rituals are performed. During these rituals, prayers are offered by holding amulets and votive items directly over the sacred flames. File:Heiden of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Interior of the Heiden File:Pictures of Heiden of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Paintings on the lattice ceiling of the Heiden
The rear of the photo shows the side of the main hall doors. File:Heiden of Taka-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Paintings on the lattice ceiling of the Heiden
The rear of the photo shows the Heiden entrance. File:Gomadan of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Goma altar at Taka Shrine
Only during the New Year period, a tree called “Nodeppō” is used as goma sticks for the ritual. '''Interior of the Worship Hall''' Inside the worship hall are housed items such as an ema depicting “Izanagi-no-Mikoto and Izanami-no-Mikoto,” paintings of hawks, and a plaque reading “Protection of the State,” dedicated in Meiji 38 to commemorate the {{jalink|Siege of Port Arthur|Q210800|Fall of Port Arthur}} during the Russo-Japanese War. File:Inside of Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine 1.JPG|Interior of the Worship Hall File:Inside of Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Interior of the Worship Hall / Entrance to the Heiden File:Ema board of Izanagi and Izanami at Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Ema of Izanagi-no-Mikoto and Izanami-no-Mikoto File:Picture of Falcon inside of Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Hawk painting
Many hawk images are dedicated because the shrine is also called “Taka Daimyōjin.” File:Board of "spiritual protection of the state" at Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|“Protection of the State” votive plaque === Other Structures === * Stone statues of the Seven Lucky Gods * Near the sacred tree behind the main hall, Buddhist statues from the era of syncretism between Shinto and Buddhism are placed. === Approach === Along the approach is a sacred pond, and on the small central island stand a small shrine of Kogota Yamatsumi Shrine and a stone monument of Konpira Shrine. The first torii gate stands southeast of {{jalink|Iwaki-Ōta Station|Q6100384}}, along the tracks of the {{jalink|Jōban Line|Q1369263}}. File:Kogota Yamatsumi-jinja shrine and Konpira-jinja shrine in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Small shrine of Kogota Yamatsumi Shrine and stone monument of Konpira Shrine File:Main approach to Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Approach File:Main approach to Taka-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Entrance to the approach of Taka Shrine File:Torii gate of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|First torii gate === Subsidiary and Branch Shrines === * Yasaka-Inari Shrine *: The shrine building reuses the former main hall of Taka Shrine, and within it are enshrined Yasaka Shrine and Inari Shrine. Yasaka Shrine was formerly called Gozu Tennō Shrine and had been located in the Jō-no-uchi area, the present location. According to *Ōsōshi*, the sacred object of Yasaka Shrine measures 7 sun in length. Inari Shrine was formerly located in the Oinari area southeast of the current site and was later enshrined together with Yasaka Shrine. *: On the east side of the shrine building are a sacred cedar tree, Raijin Shrine, and a stone monument of Aomasa Daigongen (Aomasa Shrine). * Raijin Shrine *: The sacred object is recorded as measuring 7 sun in length. * Aomasa Daigongen (Aomasa Shrine) Stone Monument *: The enshrined deities are Amaterasu Ōmikami, Tsukuyomi-no-Kami, and Ame-no-Minakanushi-no-Kami. It was transferred from {{jalink|Aomasa Shrine|Q11662833}} in Iwakiri, Miyagino Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture. File:Yasaka-Inari-jinja shrine in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Yasaka-Inari Shrine (former main hall of Taka Shrine) File:Sanctified Japanese cedar tree and raijinja shrine in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Sacred tree and Raijin Shrine File:Raijinja shrine in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Raijin Shrine File:Aoso-jinja shrine in taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone monument of Aomasa Daigongen (Aomasa Shrine) File:Statues of Shichifukujin in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone statues of the Seven Lucky Gods File:Sanctified tree and Statue of Kannon in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Sacred tree and Buddhist statue === Kifune Shrine (Ronsha of Taka Shrine) === {{Shrine |Name = Kifune Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Kifune-jinja shrine in Odaka ward,Minamisouma city. 01.JPG|270px]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisōma City|Q642094}}{{jalink|Odaka Ward|Q24892654}}2-50 Nakamachi |Coordinates = {{Wikicoord2 DMS|37|33|59.4|N|140|59|23.5|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=Kifune Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|Takaokami-no-Kami / Yamiokami-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} (trad.)
{{jalink|Takami-Musubi||Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}} (trad.)
Koyō Myōjin (trad.) |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|Myōjin Taisha}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}} |Founded = Unknown (before Genkō 3 ({{jalink|1323|Q5771}})) |Main hall = One-bay {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Annual festival = Second Sunday of January |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Kifune Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|37|33|59.4|N|140|59|23.5|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=290|coord={{coord|37|33|59.4|N|140|59|23.5|E}}}} }} It is enshrined near Sōma Odaka Shrine in Odaka Ward, Minamisōma City. The enshrined deities are Takaokami-no-Kami and Yamiokami-no-Kami. === History === The founding date of Kifune Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been worshipped since before Genkō 3 (1323). According to *Ōsōshi*, there is a record stating that the box housing the sacred object bears an inscription indicating it was made in the 5th year of Hōen, kinoe-mi year, 8th month. The sacred object is said to be a statue “riding a dragon and wearing white robes.” In Bunji 5, when {{jalink|Sōma Moritsune|Q11582495}} took control of Namekata District, he designated Kifune Shrine as the district’s chief shrine and deeply revered it, carrying out embellishments of the shrine buildings. Kifune Shrine was mainly worshipped as a deity of rainmaking and stopping rain, bountiful harvests, success and advancement in life, and martial arts. According to *Ōsōshi*, Taka Shrine once existed within the precincts of Kibune (Kifune) Shrine in Minami-Odaka Village. It was a shrine building measuring 2 shaku 5 sun in depth. It was a shrine enshrined during the era of Emperor Keikō, with Izanagi-no-Mikoto and Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto as the two main deities, and Koyō Myōjin as an accompanying deity. In Bunka 12 (1815), the lord of the Sōma Domain, {{jalink|Sōma Masutane|Q8514605}}, ordered shrine priests and a man named Watanabe Yoshitsuna to investigate and identify the eight Shikinaisha in Namekata District. Watanabe Yoshitsuna reported that the Engishiki-listed Myōjin Taisha known as Taka Shrine was the Taka Daimyōjin enshrined within the grounds of Kōmyō-ji Temple in Taka Village. In response, Takadama Tanba, a shrine priest of the Kibune Shrine in Odaka Village, submitted a counter-report claiming that Taka Shrine was a small shrine enshrined in Odaka Village. According to that report, Taka Shrine was a shrine where the Yasakani no Magatama, one of the Three Sacred Treasures, was enshrined as the local tutelary deity, and it had long been the guardian deity of Odaka Village. The ancestors of the shrine priest family originally bore the name “Takadama,” but out of deference to Taka Shrine they changed it to “Takadama.” The Taga faith’s head shrine, {{jalink|Taga Taisha|Q402091}} in Shiga Prefecture, is called “O-Taga-sama,” and the name Odaka is said to derive from calling the enshrined Taka Shrine “Mi-Taka.” The Takadama family administered the rites of both Kifune Shrine and Taka Shrine, which were enshrined near {{jalink|Sōma Odaka Shrine}} in Odaka Ward. Taka Shrine was located at a place called Nihonmatsu in Odaka Village, about ten ken from Kifune Shrine. Later, Taka Shrine was washed away by a flood during the Genroku era, but a farmer recovered the small shrine from the water and it was enshrined together within the precincts of Kifune Shrine. Takadama Tanba wrote down the above origins of Taka Shrine in Odaka Village and reported them to the domain. However, according to the compiler of *Ōsōshi*: * In shrine identification surveys conducted before the domain’s Bunka 12 investigation, Taka Shrine of Odaka Village is not recorded. * Records of the Shima family, shrine priests of Taka Shrine in Taka Village, appear in shrine priest registries from Genroku 15 and in temple and shrine surveys from the Shōtoku era, whereas records of the Takadama family only appear from Kanpō 1 onward. For these reasons, the theory of Takadama Tanba is said to contain many doubtful points. At present, the inscription on the stone monument describing the shrine’s origins at the entrance of Kifune Shrine’s approach states that the enshrined deities are Takaokami-no-Kami and Yamiokami-no-Kami, and that Taka Shrine is enshrined together in the same sanctuary. === Shrine Grounds === File:Precincts of Kifune-jinja,Minamisoma.JPG|Shrine grounds File:Haiden of Kifune-jinja after massive earthquake 11 March 2011.JPG|Worship Hall File:Honden of Kifune-jinja after massive earthquake 11 March 2011.JPG|Main Hall
Nagare-zukuri style. === Related Sites === In the Sōma region, there are said to be five Taka Shrines in total, including the two shrines described above. File:Taka-jinja shrine at Suzumigaoka Hachiman-jinja shrine.JPG|Taka Shrine, a precinct shrine within Suzumigaoka Hachiman Shrine in Sōma City === On-site Information === '''Locations''' * Taka Shrine: {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisōma City|Q642094}}{{jalink|Haramachi Ward|Q24892679}}112 Takajō-no-uchi * Kifune Shrine: {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisōma City|Q642094}}{{jalink|Odaka Ward|Q24892654}}2-50 Nakamachi === References === * Guide booklet for visiting Taka Shrine (Taka Shrine Office) * *Ōsōshi (History of Sōma City, Vol. 4)* (Sōma City, 1969) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/276003.html Taka Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation”) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Taka Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社|名たかしんしや]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]]

{{wikidata link|Q11430895}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Taka Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine in Shinto ritual season 1.JPG|270px]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisōma City|Q642094}}{{jalink|Haramachi Ward|Q24892679}}112 Takajō-no-uchi |Coordinates = {{Wikicoord2 DMS|37|36|2.81|N|140|59|33.52|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=ti|name=Taka Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} (Taka Ara-Mitama-no-Mikoto) |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|Myōjin Taisha}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}
Former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |Founded = 40th year of Emperor Keikō (12th Emperor), 7th month |Main hall = One-bay {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Alternate name = Taka Daimyōjin |Annual festival = {{jalink|April 18|Q2521}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Taka Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Taka Shrine''' (たかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Haramachi Ward|Q24892679}}, {{jalink|Minamisōma City|Q642094}}, {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}, and under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|former shrine rank}} it was designated as a {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. === Enshrined Deity === * '''{{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}''' *: According to *Ōsōshi*, a local historical record of the Sōma Domain, the shrine enshrines “Taka Ara-Mitama-no-Mikoto,” the {{jalink|Ara-mitama and Nigi-mitama|Q495568|Ara-mitama}} (rough spirit) of Izanagi-no-Mikoto. The shintai (sacred object) has been prohibited from being viewed since ancient times and is therefore unknown. There is a theory that it may be a “hawk statue,” but this hawk statue is said to be the result of a mistaken transmission identifying the “White-Token Hawk” mentioned in shrine tradition as the shintai. === History === === Overview === Taka Shrine is the foremost of the eight {{jalink|Shikinaisha||Engishiki-listed shrines}} in {{jalink|Namekata District, Fukushima|Q11626139}}, {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}} (Taka Shrine, {{jalink|Kashima Miko Shrine|Q11677124}}, {{jalink|Mitachi Shrine|Q11488802}}, {{jalink|Masutamine Shrine}}, {{jalink|Himatsuri Shrine}}, {{jalink|Takakura Shrine|Q11669904}}, {{jalink|Kanmuri Mine Shrine|Q11395049}}, {{jalink|Oshio Shrine}}). Among shrines bearing the name “Taga Shrine,” it is the only one nationwide to have been granted the rank of {{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964}}, and it is said that Taga Shrines throughout the Tōhoku region originate from this shrine. Although its exact origins are unknown, tradition holds that the shrine was founded in the 40th year of {{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}} by {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}}, who visited this area during his eastern campaign and enshrined the deity to pray for victory in battle. To the northwest of the current location lies the Furuuchi area, through which the Ōta River flows, and there is said to be a place called “Daimyōjin Kawara.” The ancient shrine site is said to have been on the southern hills of Daimyōjin Kawara. In the 7th year, 2nd month of {{jalink|Emperor Chūai|Q179971}}, a violent storm washed away and severely damaged the shrine buildings, which drifted to the banks of the nearby Ōta River. In the 9th month of the same year, the shrine was relocated to Ashinodaira (present-day Jō-no-uchi, the current site). In Kōryaku 2 ({{jalink|1380|Q6327}}), there existed a bronze bell (waniguchi) bearing an inscription of donation by Fujiwara Yoshimori, which is said to hang in the inner sanctuary of the main hall. From April of Jōō 4, Kinoe-mi year ({{jalink|1655|Q6975}}) to Meireki 1 (the same year), the shrine sanctuary was repaired, with timber donated by {{jalink|Sōma Tadatane|Q8514579}}. During the {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} era, {{jalink|Sōma Masatane|Q8514599}} built a sanctuary called “Yōshinden,” associated with {{jalink|Yoshida Shintō|Q1760351}}, within the castle grounds, and at that time, for certain reasons, had a statue of the “White-Token Hawk” carved and dedicated to Taka Shrine. This wooden statue of the “White-Token Hawk” is said to have been stored in a miniature shrine. Even today, the “White-Token Hawk Statue” is enshrined in the main hall as a sacred treasure. According to shrine tradition, although it appears to be an ordinary wooden statue, it was named “White-Token Hawk” because the feathers appear white depending on how the light strikes them. In Kyōhō 9 ({{jalink|1724|Q6926}}), Sōma Masatane donated shrine lands amounting to 1 koku and 9 to, among other offerings, and the shrine received deep reverence from successive lords of the Sōma Domain. In Meiji 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was designated a village shrine, and in Shōwa 19 ({{jalink|1944|Q5268}}), it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. Taka Shrine has long been believed to bestow divine benefits such as longevity, fulfillment of wishes, household safety, safe childbirth, maritime safety, and abundant catches. Because it was originally enshrined to pray for victory in battle, many worshippers in particular visit to pray for certain victory in elections. === Court Rank === * Enryaku 15 ({{jalink|796|Q33452}}) 10th month, 22nd day: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (*{{jalink|Nihon Kōki|Q1077029}}*) – recorded as “Taga Shrine.” === Shrine Grounds === === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Built in one-bay nagare-zukuri style, with side screens and balustrades. Dragon carvings are applied to the side screens. File:Honden of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Main Hall File:Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine in Shinto ritual season 2.JPG|Worship Hall '''Interior of the Heiden''' The coffered lattice ceiling of the heiden is decorated with polychrome paintings of animals, plants, and human figures. Only during the first three days of the New Year, a goma altar is set up in front of the main hall doors within the heiden, where goma fire rituals are performed. During these rituals, prayers are offered by holding amulets and votive items directly over the sacred flames. File:Heiden of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Interior of the Heiden File:Pictures of Heiden of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Paintings on the lattice ceiling of the Heiden
The rear of the photo shows the side of the main hall doors. File:Heiden of Taka-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Paintings on the lattice ceiling of the Heiden
The rear of the photo shows the Heiden entrance. File:Gomadan of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Goma altar at Taka Shrine
Only during the New Year period, a tree called “Nodeppō” is used as goma sticks for the ritual. '''Interior of the Worship Hall''' Inside the worship hall are housed items such as an ema depicting “Izanagi-no-Mikoto and Izanami-no-Mikoto,” paintings of hawks, and a plaque reading “Protection of the State,” dedicated in Meiji 38 to commemorate the {{jalink|Siege of Port Arthur|Q210800|Fall of Port Arthur}} during the Russo-Japanese War. File:Inside of Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine 1.JPG|Interior of the Worship Hall File:Inside of Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Interior of the Worship Hall / Entrance to the Heiden File:Ema board of Izanagi and Izanami at Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Ema of Izanagi-no-Mikoto and Izanami-no-Mikoto File:Picture of Falcon inside of Haiden of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Hawk painting
Many hawk images are dedicated because the shrine is also called “Taka Daimyōjin.” File:Board of "spiritual protection of the state" at Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|“Protection of the State” votive plaque === Other Structures === * Stone statues of the Seven Lucky Gods * Near the sacred tree behind the main hall, Buddhist statues from the era of syncretism between Shinto and Buddhism are placed. === Approach === Along the approach is a sacred pond, and on the small central island stand a small shrine of Kogota Yamatsumi Shrine and a stone monument of Konpira Shrine. The first torii gate stands southeast of {{jalink|Iwaki-Ōta Station|Q6100384}}, along the tracks of the {{jalink|Jōban Line|Q1369263}}. File:Kogota Yamatsumi-jinja shrine and Konpira-jinja shrine in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Small shrine of Kogota Yamatsumi Shrine and stone monument of Konpira Shrine File:Main approach to Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Approach File:Main approach to Taka-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Entrance to the approach of Taka Shrine File:Torii gate of Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|First torii gate === Subsidiary and Branch Shrines === * Yasaka-Inari Shrine *: The shrine building reuses the former main hall of Taka Shrine, and within it are enshrined Yasaka Shrine and Inari Shrine. Yasaka Shrine was formerly called Gozu Tennō Shrine and had been located in the Jō-no-uchi area, the present location. According to *Ōsōshi*, the sacred object of Yasaka Shrine measures 7 sun in length. Inari Shrine was formerly located in the Oinari area southeast of the current site and was later enshrined together with Yasaka Shrine. *: On the east side of the shrine building are a sacred cedar tree, Raijin Shrine, and a stone monument of Aomasa Daigongen (Aomasa Shrine). * Raijin Shrine *: The sacred object is recorded as measuring 7 sun in length. * Aomasa Daigongen (Aomasa Shrine) Stone Monument *: The enshrined deities are Amaterasu Ōmikami, Tsukuyomi-no-Kami, and Ame-no-Minakanushi-no-Kami. It was transferred from {{jalink|Aomasa Shrine|Q11662833}} in Iwakiri, Miyagino Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture. File:Yasaka-Inari-jinja shrine in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Yasaka-Inari Shrine (former main hall of Taka Shrine) File:Sanctified Japanese cedar tree and raijinja shrine in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Sacred tree and Raijin Shrine File:Raijinja shrine in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Raijin Shrine File:Aoso-jinja shrine in taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone monument of Aomasa Daigongen (Aomasa Shrine) File:Statues of Shichifukujin in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone statues of the Seven Lucky Gods File:Sanctified tree and Statue of Kannon in Taka-jinja shrine.JPG|Sacred tree and Buddhist statue === Kifune Shrine (Ronsha of Taka Shrine) === {{Shrine |Name = Kifune Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Kifune-jinja shrine in Odaka ward,Minamisouma city. 01.JPG|270px]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisōma City|Q642094}}{{jalink|Odaka Ward|Q24892654}}2-50 Nakamachi |Coordinates = {{Wikicoord2 DMS|37|33|59.4|N|140|59|23.5|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=Kifune Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|Takaokami-no-Kami / Yamiokami-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} (trad.)
{{jalink|Takami-Musubi||Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}} (trad.)
Koyō Myōjin (trad.) |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|Myōjin Taisha}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}} |Founded = Unknown (before Genkō 3 ({{jalink|1323|Q5771}})) |Main hall = One-bay {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Annual festival = Second Sunday of January |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Kifune Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|37|33|59.4|N|140|59|23.5|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=290|coord={{coord|37|33|59.4|N|140|59|23.5|E}}}} }} It is enshrined near Sōma Odaka Shrine in Odaka Ward, Minamisōma City. The enshrined deities are Takaokami-no-Kami and Yamiokami-no-Kami. === History === The founding date of Kifune Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have been worshipped since before Genkō 3 (1323). According to *Ōsōshi*, there is a record stating that the box housing the sacred object bears an inscription indicating it was made in the 5th year of Hōen, kinoe-mi year, 8th month. The sacred object is said to be a statue “riding a dragon and wearing white robes.” In Bunji 5, when {{jalink|Sōma Moritsune|Q11582495}} took control of Namekata District, he designated Kifune Shrine as the district’s chief shrine and deeply revered it, carrying out embellishments of the shrine buildings. Kifune Shrine was mainly worshipped as a deity of rainmaking and stopping rain, bountiful harvests, success and advancement in life, and martial arts. According to *Ōsōshi*, Taka Shrine once existed within the precincts of Kibune (Kifune) Shrine in Minami-Odaka Village. It was a shrine building measuring 2 shaku 5 sun in depth. It was a shrine enshrined during the era of Emperor Keikō, with Izanagi-no-Mikoto and Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto as the two main deities, and Koyō Myōjin as an accompanying deity. In Bunka 12 (1815), the lord of the Sōma Domain, {{jalink|Sōma Masutane|Q8514605}}, ordered shrine priests and a man named Watanabe Yoshitsuna to investigate and identify the eight Shikinaisha in Namekata District. Watanabe Yoshitsuna reported that the Engishiki-listed Myōjin Taisha known as Taka Shrine was the Taka Daimyōjin enshrined within the grounds of Kōmyō-ji Temple in Taka Village. In response, Takadama Tanba, a shrine priest of the Kibune Shrine in Odaka Village, submitted a counter-report claiming that Taka Shrine was a small shrine enshrined in Odaka Village. According to that report, Taka Shrine was a shrine where the Yasakani no Magatama, one of the Three Sacred Treasures, was enshrined as the local tutelary deity, and it had long been the guardian deity of Odaka Village. The ancestors of the shrine priest family originally bore the name “Takadama,” but out of deference to Taka Shrine they changed it to “Takadama.” The Taga faith’s head shrine, {{jalink|Taga Taisha|Q402091}} in Shiga Prefecture, is called “O-Taga-sama,” and the name Odaka is said to derive from calling the enshrined Taka Shrine “Mi-Taka.” The Takadama family administered the rites of both Kifune Shrine and Taka Shrine, which were enshrined near {{jalink|Sōma Odaka Shrine}} in Odaka Ward. Taka Shrine was located at a place called Nihonmatsu in Odaka Village, about ten ken from Kifune Shrine. Later, Taka Shrine was washed away by a flood during the Genroku era, but a farmer recovered the small shrine from the water and it was enshrined together within the precincts of Kifune Shrine. Takadama Tanba wrote down the above origins of Taka Shrine in Odaka Village and reported them to the domain. However, according to the compiler of *Ōsōshi*: * In shrine identification surveys conducted before the domain’s Bunka 12 investigation, Taka Shrine of Odaka Village is not recorded. * Records of the Shima family, shrine priests of Taka Shrine in Taka Village, appear in shrine priest registries from Genroku 15 and in temple and shrine surveys from the Shōtoku era, whereas records of the Takadama family only appear from Kanpō 1 onward. For these reasons, the theory of Takadama Tanba is said to contain many doubtful points. At present, the inscription on the stone monument describing the shrine’s origins at the entrance of Kifune Shrine’s approach states that the enshrined deities are Takaokami-no-Kami and Yamiokami-no-Kami, and that Taka Shrine is enshrined together in the same sanctuary. === Shrine Grounds === File:Precincts of Kifune-jinja,Minamisoma.JPG|Shrine grounds File:Haiden of Kifune-jinja after massive earthquake 11 March 2011.JPG|Worship Hall File:Honden of Kifune-jinja after massive earthquake 11 March 2011.JPG|Main Hall
Nagare-zukuri style. === Related Sites === In the Sōma region, there are said to be five Taka Shrines in total, including the two shrines described above. File:Taka-jinja shrine at Suzumigaoka Hachiman-jinja shrine.JPG|Taka Shrine, a precinct shrine within Suzumigaoka Hachiman Shrine in Sōma City === On-site Information === '''Locations''' * Taka Shrine: {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisōma City|Q642094}}{{jalink|Haramachi Ward|Q24892679}}112 Takajō-no-uchi * Kifune Shrine: {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisōma City|Q642094}}{{jalink|Odaka Ward|Q24892654}}2-50 Nakamachi === References === * Guide booklet for visiting Taka Shrine (Taka Shrine Office) * *Ōsōshi (History of Sōma City, Vol. 4)* (Sōma City, 1969) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/276003.html Taka Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation”) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Taka Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社|名たかしんしや]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]]
